{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents discuss the causes of drug-induced gingival overgrowth (DIGO). Phenytoin, sodium valproate, phenobarbitone, vigabatrin, primidone, mephenytoin, and ethosuximide are mentioned as anticonvulsants that cause gingival hypertrophy. Carbamazepine is noted to have a lower rate of gingival enlargement compared to phenytoin and valproic acid. Verapamil is mentioned to exhibit a lower prevalence of gingival enlargement compared to nifedipine. Bleomycin is not mentioned in relation to gingival hyperplasia. Therefore, Bleomycin is the exception.", "answer_choice": "D"}
